mnemonics: PINNS: Primitive Is Not Non-primitive. Primitive types are the Numbers and basic building blocks (like Integers, Numbers, Null, Strings - PINNS). Non-primitive types are built from these primitives.
keyPoints: Primitive data structures are fundamental types directly operated upon by machine instructions (e.g., integer, float, char, pointer).
Non-primitive data structures are derived from primitive types and organize data for efficient processing (e.g., arrays, lists, stacks, queues, trees, graphs).
Non-primitive structures are further classified into linear (sequential organization) and non-linear (hierarchical or network organization).
commonMistakes: Confusing the classification level (e.g., thinking 'array' is primitive because it's common, when it is actually a non-primitive linear structure).
Assuming all non-primitive structures are complex; some, like arrays, are simple linear organizations.
